text: Refactoring is the process of improving the internal structure of code without changing how it behaves. Instead of adding new features, developers focus on making the code cleaner and easier to understand. For example, they might rename confusing variables, break long functions into smaller ones, or remove duplicate code. These changes do not affect what the program does, but they make it more readable and maintainable. Over time, code that is not refactored can accumulate what is known as technical debt. This debt makes future changes slower and riskier. By regularly refactoring, teams keep their codebase healthy and reduce the cost of future modifications.
